description:
## Goal

Bridge opaque entry to specialized refinements. When an AOT-emitted
canonical body has hot refinements available AND is reached through
opaque channels (closure dispatch, spawn, scheduler resume), insert
a dispatch prelude at canonical entry that type-tests args and
return_calls into the matching refinement.

Router pays cost ONCE on opaque entry. Direct calls bypass it (they
emit straight to refinement). Recursion inside refinements stays
direct — the router never runs in a hot loop.

## What lands

- Router-insertion predicate in codegen:
  ```
  fn should_insert_router(canonical: &Canonical) -> bool {
      let opaque_entries = count_opaque_entries(canonical.fid);
      let emitted_refinements = canonical.refinements.iter()
          .filter(|r| r.emit).count();
      opaque_entries > 0 && emitted_refinements > 0
  }
  ```

  Opaque entries:
  - canonical.fid is body_fn_id of some MakeClosure with
    unresolved closure_lit.
  - canonical.fid is a spawn target.
  - canonical.fid is reachable via scheduler resume (mid-flight
    or parked-cont continuation).

- Dispatch prelude generation:
  - Inserted as the canonical body's entry block.
  - For each emitted refinement, ordered by subsumption-minimal
    match, emit a branch sequence:
    ```
    block0(v0: i64, v1: i64, ...):
        ; refinement R1: narrowed_key matches if v0 is rawint AND v1 is rawint
        v_test1 = type_test(v0, RawInt)
        v_test2 = type_test(v1, RawInt)
        v_match1 = band v_test1, v_test2
        brif v_match1, route_R1, check_R2

    route_R1:
        ; coerce per-arg from Tagged to refinement-expected repr
        v0_raw = untag_int(v0)
        v1_raw = untag_int(v1)
        return_call fn_R1_sid(v0_raw, v1_raw)

    check_R2: ...

    fallthrough_canonical:
        ; original canonical body's first block becomes this
    ```

- Coercion sequence emitted per branch uses
  ArgRepr::from_descr-driven cast library:
  Tagged→RawInt = shift, Tagged→RawF64 = pointer load,
  RawInt→Tagged = shift+or, RawF64→Tagged = heap alloc+store.

- Order: refinements sorted by subsumption-minimal first
  (most-specific narrowing checked first; fallthrough catches
  broader). Same ordering rule as SpecRegistry::resolve.

- Direct calls to canonical (when annotation didn't refine to
  a refinement) emit `return_call fn_canonical(...)` and ENTER
  AT block1 (the original canonical entry, now post-router).
  Codegen tracks the post-router block id and routes direct
  callsites there. Router-entry is opaque-only.

## What gets removed

Nothing structural. The router is opt-in; canonical bodies
without router-eligibility stay unchanged.

## Expected effect

- Opaque-entry-heavy fns (closure-recursion patterns like map,
  filter, each over lists) get free specialization via the
  router. Loops run narrow.
- Code size on AOT grows slightly (the prelude); offset by
  refinements being reached by paths that would otherwise stay
  canonical.

## Acceptance

- A fixture with closure-recursion over a typed list (e.g., the
  fz-t45 repro with each + closure-with-captures) shows the
  router prelude in expected.clif.aot and produces correct
  output.
- Direct calls within fixtures' fns stay direct — no router
  cost on recursive narrow-spec hot loops.
- Spec-quality diagnostic reports router insertions.

## See also

- fz-rfn.3 — emission policy + golden split (blocking).
- fz-t45 — closes definitively here (was already structurally
  dissolved in rfn.1; the router makes the closure→specialized
  path optimal).
title: fz-rfn.4: canonical-with-router for AOT opaque entry
